name: Bouillabaisse
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 lbs. various fish (such as Mediterranean fish like scorpion fish, capon, conger eel, cicada, etc.)
12 potatoes, peeled and cut into thick slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the fish in a large pot.
2. Add the saffron, olive oil, and mix well.
3. Sprinkle salt and pepper over the mixture.
4. Let soak for 1-2 hours.
5. Add the tomatoes, potatoes, and water to cover. Bring to a full boil.
6. When the fish is cooked (10-15 minutes), use a skimmer to remove it and the potatoes into a dish.
7. Serve the bouillon with croutons made from stale bread, and sprinkle with minced garlic and rouille sauce.
Note: This recipe does not specify what to do with all the fish and potatoes after cooking. Many people simply discard any remaining scraps.
